pattern: Tracking a set of non-overlapping intervals and rejecting any new interval that intersects an existing one is the core of single-booking scheduling.
hints: Two half-open intervals overlap exactly when each one starts strictly before the other ends.
Before accepting a booking, you must confirm it touches none of the intervals already accepted.
Keep the accepted intervals and, for each request, test it against them; if it clears all of them, store it and answer true, otherwise answer false without storing.

edge_inputs.description: statement example: middle event collides, the touching-at-endpoint event is allowed
shared endpoint does not count as overlap for half-open intervals
a fully contained interval is still a double booking and is rejected
an interval that strictly contains an existing one is rejected too
exact duplicate booking is a collision

stress.description: many random bookings over a small coordinate space so collisions are frequent
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    ops = ['MyCalendar']
    args = [[]]
    for _ in range(max(1, size)):
        s = rng.randint(0, 80)
        e = s + rng.randint(1, 20)
        ops.append('book'); args.append([s, e])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: dense overlaps exercise both the accept and reject branches
